description: Ground-truth labels for the no-op detector (scripts/noop-scan.js + core/skills/noop-detect.js). Each case is a single SKILL.md line (or a representative synthetic line). The detector is calibrated and regression-tested against this set by scripts/test-noop-detect.js. A 'no-op' is an instruction aimed at the agent's own disposition/effort with no specific, checkable criterion: deleting it does not change agent behavior (Pocock's ablation test).

labels.noop: Whole line is inert disposition/effort filler. Fix: delete it. Detector MUST flag.
labels.noop-fragment: Line carries a real instruction but contains trimmable filler (e.g. a trailing adverb). Fix: trim the filler, keep the instruction. Detector SHOULD flag (judge decides trim-vs-delete).
labels.load-bearing: Concrete, checkable instruction (command, file, threshold, named action). Fix: none. Detector MUST NOT flag.
labels.fringe-load-bearing: A guard for a missing/absent/error condition. Removing it deletes a real safeguard. Detector MUST NOT flag (critical exemption - a false positive here is the most dangerous failure mode).

cases.text: Be thorough in your analysis.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: Pure disposition. Agents already attempt thoroughness; the line adds no checkable criterion.

cases.text: Make the commit message very detailed.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: Effort directive, no criterion for 'detailed enough'. Agents already write detailed commit messages.

cases.text: Make the implementation easy to read.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: Disposition, not a constraint. No definition of 'easy to read' that changes behavior.

cases.text: Always try your best to produce high-quality output.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: Stacked filler: 'try your best' + 'high-quality'. Zero checkable content.

cases.text: Be careful and pay attention to detail.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: Disposition only; no object, no criterion.

cases.text: Ensure the code is clean and well-structured.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'clean', 'well-structured' are unmeasurable. No anchor, no condition.

cases.text: Strive to write robust, production-grade code.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'strive', 'robust', 'production-grade' all vague. No checkable bar.

cases.text: Remember to be comprehensive.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'remember to' + 'comprehensive': pure reminder with no content.

cases.text: Take care to handle things properly.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'take care', 'properly' with no object. Maximally inert.

cases.text: Aim to make the output as clear as possible.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'aim to', 'as clear as possible' - aspirational, not operational.

cases.text: It's important to write good code.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'it's important', 'good' - meta-commentary, no instruction.

cases.text: Do your best to be helpful and thorough.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'do your best', 'thorough' - disposition stack.

cases.text: Make sure everything works correctly and seamlessly.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'make sure', 'correctly', 'seamlessly' with no named check.

cases.text: Keep the user experience smooth and intuitive.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'smooth', 'intuitive' - unmeasurable UX adjectives.

cases.text: Read the error message, stack trace, or bug description thoroughly
cases.label: noop-fragment
cases.rationale: The instruction (read the error message / stack trace / bug description) is load-bearing; only the trailing 'thoroughly' is filler. Judge should trim the adverb, keep the line.

cases.text: Familiar (5+ sessions): confirms deletions before executing; novices should review suggestions carefully before confirming
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Carries session-tier logic ('5+ sessions') and a confirmation gate ('before confirming'). The numeric anchor and concrete gate make the line load-bearing; the lone 'carefully' is not worth a false-positive risk against an anchored line. The detector correctly declines to flag it.

cases.text: Write a thorough and detailed summary of the changes.
cases.label: noop-fragment
cases.rationale: 'Write a summary of the changes' is real; 'thorough and detailed' is filler to trim.

cases.text: Carefully run the migration and verify the row counts match.
cases.label: noop-fragment
cases.rationale: 'run the migration and verify row counts match' is checkable and load-bearing; leading 'Carefully' is trimmable filler.

cases.text: Run `node scripts/test-all.js` before shipping.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Names an exact command and a gate. Deleting it removes a real step. Inline code anchor.

cases.text: Keep individual phases under 35 minutes of execution time.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Numeric, checkable threshold. Anchored by '35 minutes'.

cases.text: Output a HANDOFF block: 3-5 bullets, under 150 words.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Specific format constraints (3-5 bullets, 150 words). Measurable.

cases.text: Every phase must have a verifiable exit condition -- binary pass/fail signals (typecheck, tests, build).
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Names concrete signals (typecheck, tests, build) and a hard requirement. No filler.

cases.text: Run `node scripts/skill-lint.js {name}` to verify structure.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Exact command, clear purpose. Inline code anchor.

cases.text: Be thorough: run `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, and `npm run build`, and fix every failure.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Contains the phrase 'be thorough' BUT is anchored by three concrete commands and a checkable action ('fix every failure'). Tests that an anchor exempts the vocab hit. Deleting it removes real steps.

cases.text: Make sure `.env` is listed in `.gitignore` before committing.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Contains 'make sure' BUT names a concrete, checkable file condition (`.env` in `.gitignore`). Anchor exempts the vocab.

cases.text: Subscribe returns a cleanup function - always call it.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Specific, checkable behavior. No disposition filler.

cases.text: Use stable keys for dynamic lists: `key={item.id}` - never `key={i}` when the list can change.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Concrete rule with code anchor and a named anti-pattern.

cases.text: Trim by deleting, not by compressing prose into ambiguity.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: A specific directive about HOW to trim. Carries real, non-obvious guidance an agent would not default to.

cases.text: Pass only distilled findings back to the orchestrator, never raw tool output.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Concrete constraint on what to return. Changes behavior.

cases.text: `.planning/` does not exist: skip Tier 1 gracefully and fall through to Tier 2.
cases.label: fringe-load-bearing
cases.rationale: A guard for a missing directory. 'gracefully' here is a guard signal, not filler. Removing it deletes the missing-state path.

cases.text: Screenshot tool unavailable (Playwright not installed): output what to check manually and exit gracefully.
cases.label: fringe-load-bearing
cases.rationale: Guard for an unavailable tool. The whole line is the safeguard.

cases.text: No UI (API-only project): Report that /qa requires a browser-accessible interface, then stop gracefully.
cases.label: fringe-load-bearing
cases.rationale: Guard for the no-UI case. Removing it makes the skill crash or misbehave on API-only projects.

cases.text: If the gh CLI is not installed or not authenticated, surface an install/auth hint instead of failing silently.
cases.label: fringe-load-bearing
cases.rationale: Guard for a missing dependency. 'if not installed' is a condition, not disposition.

cases.text: When `.planning/campaigns/` is absent, treat it as empty and show the setup hint.
cases.label: fringe-load-bearing
cases.rationale: Missing-state guard with a concrete fallback ('treat as empty', 'show setup hint').

cases.text: Ensure the dev server is running (start it if not).
cases.label: fringe-load-bearing
cases.rationale: Contains 'Ensure' BUT carries a concrete conditional action ('start it if not'). The 'if not' guard plus the concrete action make this load-bearing, not filler.

cases.text: Community/GitHub query (e.g., "express error middleware best practices site:github.com").
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Contains 'best practices' BUT it is a literal search-query EXAMPLE (note 'site:'), not an instruction to the agent. The site: marker and e.g. exempt it.

cases.text: PASS: renders correctly, matches expectations.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: A rubric/label line (verdict definition), not an instruction. 'correctly' is part of a PASS-criteria description, not effort filler.

cases.text: Source of truth: `useDatabase` (Zustand + IDB).
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Names a concrete API. Informational anchor, no filler.

cases.text: Define event types in `src/kernel/system/eventBus.ts`.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Exact file path. Concrete, checkable.

cases.text: Skills live in `skills/`. Each skill is a directory `skills/{name}/SKILL.md`.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Structural fact with path anchors. No disposition.

cases.text: Handle all the edge cases properly and make it robust.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'edge cases' sounds concrete but 'properly' and 'robust' carry no named case or criterion - pure hand-wave. Contrast with the fringe-load-bearing guards that name the actual condition.

cases.text: Follow best practices and industry standards throughout.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: 'best practices', 'industry standards' with no named practice. Inert (no site: example, no concrete rule).

cases.text: Write clean, maintainable, well-documented code.
cases.label: noop
cases.rationale: Adjective stack with no measurable target.

cases.text: Use TypeScript strict mode.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Specific, checkable setting. No filler despite being short.

cases.text: Prefer `const` over `let`, never use `var`.
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Concrete, enforceable rule with named tokens.

cases.text: Recommendations are concrete actions (not "be more careful").
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Contains 'careful' BUT it is cited as a NEGATED anti-example of what a recommendation must not be. A negated disposition word is the opposite of an instruction. Regression case for the negation guard.

cases.text: Stop at 3-6 credible sources (not exhaustive - focused).
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Contains 'exhaustive' BUT negated ('not exhaustive') and anchored by '3-6 sources'. The line is a concrete stopping rule. Regression case for negation + the sources/queries numeric anchor.

cases.text: Search exhaustively (2-4 queries, 3-6 sources per agent, done).
cases.label: load-bearing
cases.rationale: Contains 'exhaustively' BUT immediately bounded by concrete counts (2-4 queries, 3-6 sources). The numbers define exactly what 'exhaustively' means here, so it is load-bearing, not filler.
